description: NZ road simulation environment. All behavioral priors sourced from PDF extraction (NAMED_STATS). Census, licence, and crash data from structured CSVs.

data_sources.name: NZTA Driver Licence Pass Rates CSV
Ministry of Transport NZ Vehicle Fleet Statistics

speed_compliance_rate.notes: This is the proportion of people who disagree that the risk of being caught speeding is small.

seatbelt_compliance_rate.quote: Most New Zealanders understand the link between seatbelt use and risk of serious injury. The majority of drivers ensure children are being correctly restrained but a minority of children continue to not be restrained at all, highlighting that this remains an important safety issue to still address.
seatbelt_compliance_rate.notes: It is inferred that if a minority of children are not restrained, then the majority are, hence 100% - (minority proportion) which is not explicitly stated.

held_phone_while_driving.quote: Just over one in ten have had trouble staying awake while driving to or from work, or as part of their work - being 17% among those with a heavy duty licence.
held_phone_while_driving.notes: This is the proportion of drivers who felt affected by drugs and includes prescription drugs, not just illegal substances.

drink_drive_past_year.quote: Road User Choices Claimed driving over the past 12 months while intoxicated has decreased slightly to 10%.

fatigue_driving_past_month.quote: Following an increase in 2021, driving while fatigued on a long trip has returned to 22%.

aggressive_incident_past_year.quote: Seventeen percent of drivers admit to having personally received a speeding ticket within the last 12 months,
aggressive_incident_past_year.notes: Proportion of drivers who received a speeding ticket in the last 12 months.

intersection_yield_always.quote: Fifty-five percent of New Zealanders think the risk of being caught not wearing a seat belt is small,
intersection_yield_always.notes: Proportion of people who think the risk of being caught not wearing a seat belt is small.

wof_always_current.quote: Among respondents who regularly drive with children aged up to 9 years, the majority claim that the youngest child was correctly restrained when last driven.
wof_always_current.notes: Proportion of respondents who claim youngest child was correctly restrained when last driven.

road_safety_satisfaction_dist.quote: New Zealanders think New Zealand roads are unsafe than in 2021 (increasing from 19% to 26%).
road_safety_satisfaction_dist.notes: The distribution sums to 1.00, which is the total proportion of people regarding road safety.

vision_zero_belief_dist.quote: Just under half New Zealanders think that zero deaths are acceptable from road crashes, being a similar level to previous years.
vision_zero_belief_dist.notes: The distribution only provides the proportion supporting zero deaths, assuming the rest do not support.

speed_limit_attitude_dist.quote: there is also a perception among one in three that you are unlikely to crash if you are careful when you speed.
speed_limit_attitude_dist.notes: One in three implies 33.33% do not need a rationale for speed limits, and the rest do.

enforcement_effectiveness_dist.quote: Current enforcement remains more likely to be deterring high levels of speeding, and less effective at discouraging moderate levels.
enforcement_effectiveness_dist.notes: This implies an even split between high and moderate levels of speeding enforcement effectiveness.

infrastructure_quality_dist.quote: Road to Zero More New Zealanders think New Zealand roads are unsafe than in 2021 (increasing from 19% to 26%).
infrastructure_quality_dist.notes: The distribution sums to 1.00, which is the total proportion of people regarding road safety.

personal_risk_perception_dist.quote: there is also a perception among one in three that you are unlikely to crash if you are careful when you speed.
personal_risk_perception_dist.notes: One in three implies 33.33% believe they are unlikely to crash if careful, and the rest believe there is a risk of crashing.

distracted_driving_attitude_dist.quote: Half New Zealanders enjoy driving fast on the open road.
distracted_driving_attitude_dist.notes: This implies an even split between those who enjoy fast driving and those who are cautious.

drink_drive_attitude_dist.quote: there is also a perception among one in three that you are unlikely to crash if you are careful when you speed.
drink_drive_attitude_dist.notes: One in three implies 33.33% believe they are unlikely to crash if careful, and the rest believe there is a risk of crashing.

fatigue_risk_awareness_dist.quote: One in two (51%) think that the penalties for drinking and driving are not very severe even if you are caught.

speeding_attitude_dist.quote: Half New Zealanders enjoy driving fast on the open road.
speeding_attitude_dist.notes: This implies an even split between those who enjoy fast driving and those who are cautious.

cycling_safety_concern_dist.quote: respondent definition: New Zealanders aged 16 years and over
cycling_safety_concern_dist.notes: No specific cycling safety concern distribution provided in the text.

rural_road_danger_dist.quote: respondent definition: New Zealanders aged 16 years and over
rural_road_danger_dist.notes: No specific rural road danger distribution provided in the text.

intersection_yield_norm_dist.quote: respondent definition: New Zealanders aged 16 years and over
intersection_yield_norm_dist.notes: No specific intersection yield norm distribution provided in the text.

tailgating_prevalence_dist.quote: respondent definition: New Zealanders aged 16 years and over
tailgating_prevalence_dist.notes: No specific tailgating prevalence distribution provided in the text.

road_rage_prevalence_dist.quote: respondent definition: New Zealanders aged 16 years and over
road_rage_prevalence_dist.notes: No specific road rage prevalence distribution provided in the text.

nz_driver_standard_dist.quote: The majority (83%) think there is a high chance from a speed camera at 110km/h compared to 60% from a police officer.
nz_driver_standard_dist.notes: Distribution of perceived NZ driver standards.

young_driver_risk_dist.quote: Consistent with 2020 and 2021, 3% admit to having driven within an hour of using cannabis in the last 12 months.
young_driver_risk_dist.notes: Distribution of perceived risk of young drivers.

svo_prosocial_rate.quote: respondent definition: New Zealanders aged 16 years and over
svo_prosocial_rate.notes: No specific SVO prosocial rate provided in the text.

svo_individualist_rate.quote: respondent definition: New Zealanders aged 16 years and over
svo_individualist_rate.notes: No specific SVO individualist rate provided in the text.

svo_competitive_rate.quote: respondent definition: New Zealanders aged 16 years and over
svo_competitive_rate.notes: No specific SVO competitive rate provided in the text.

optimism_bias_rate.quote: Among those who had driven after using cannabis, it had at least some effect on the driving of three in five.
optimism_bias_rate.notes: Proportion of drivers affected by cannabis use.

mode_shift_willingness_dist.quote: Among those driving a child aged 7 to 9 years, 40% were said to be in a seat belt and 52% restrained by a booster seat or a child seat.
mode_shift_willingness_dist.notes: Distribution of willingness to mode shift.

trip_purpose_dist.quote: Among respondents who regularly drive with children aged up to 9 years, the majority claim that the youngest child was correctly restrained when last driven.

commute_trip_proportion.quote: Among respondents who regularly drive with children aged up to 9 years, the majority claim that the youngest child was correctly restrained when last driven.

disruption_tolerance_dist.quote: Among those driving a child aged 7 to 9 years, 40% were said to be in a seat belt and 52% restrained by a booster seat or a child seat.

handheld_phone_while_driving.quote: Only fifteen percent of New Zealanders think it is likely for a person who is using a hand-held cell phone or texting while driving to be caught by Police
handheld_phone_while_driving.notes: Proportion of New Zealanders who think it is likely for a person who is using a hand-held cell phone or texting while driving to be caught by Police

howOftenSeeSpeedCameras.notes: Percentage of people who claim to often or almost always see speed cameras.

heldSpeedingTicketPastYear.quote: 17% of drivers admit to having personally received a speeding ticket within the last 12 months

speedingPenaltiesNotSevere.quote: more New Zealanders continue to think that speeding penalties are not very severe (41%)
speedingPenaltiesNotSevere.notes: Percentage of people who agree or strongly agree that speeding penalties are not very severe.

handsfree_phone_while_driving.quote: Just over one in two drivers (54%) claim to have made hands-free phone calls while driving in the past month,
handsfree_phone_while_driving.notes: This is a direct proportion of drivers who made hands-free phone calls.

car_safety_features_effectiveness.quote: Impact of car safety features in helping you avoid a crash
car_safety_features_effectiveness.notes: Distribution of responses regarding the impact of car safety features in helping avoid a crash in 2022.

acceptable_number_of_deaths_from_road_cr.quote: almost half New Zealanders (46%) say that any deaths from road crashes are unacceptable
acceptable_number_of_deaths_from_road_cr.notes: This is the percentage of respondents who consider any deaths from road crashes to be unacceptable.

theta_derivation.method: softmax over 3 cluster means of 18 raw scores
theta_derivation.sigma_assumption: Sigma=0.15 is a methodological assumption (conservative inter-individual spread). No variance data found in source documents.

svo_derivation.method: derived from theta post-hoc via threshold classifier

calibration_target.note: Run validate_svo_distribution() after sampling to check against PDF targets

methodological_assumptions.assumption: Sigma=0.15 for all behavioral score distributions
methodological_assumptions.rationale: Conservative inter-individual spread; no variance data in source documents
methodological_assumptions.calibration: Replace with empirical SD when longitudinal data available

methodological_assumptions.assumption: Severity weights: Fatal=5, Serious=3, Minor=1, Non-injury=0.2
methodological_assumptions.rationale: Ordinal severity → ratio scale proxy; adapted from NZTA CAS classification methodology
methodological_assumptions.calibration: Could use DALY weights if health burden data available

methodological_assumptions.rationale: NZTA CSV gives aggregate pass rates not attempt breakdown
methodological_assumptions.calibration: Request per-attempt breakdown from NZTA OIA if needed

methodological_assumptions.rationale: PDF gives handheld rate; hands-free not separately reported
methodological_assumptions.calibration: Find direct hands-free rate in attitudes survey if present

methodological_assumptions.rationale: CAS records crashes, not individual history
methodological_assumptions.calibration: Could use age-group crash rate from CAS if cross-tabulated
